Virtual, 1) The INDIVIDUAL MERITS DATE is 5/5/2026 @ 2:30 PM. 30 day call-up. WebEx is
acceptable provided Respondent is in counsel's office.
2) Respondent must submit an update E-33 within ten days of this Order.
3) Respondent specifically confirmed that she is not filing an I-589. Based upon the
conversation on the record at the MCH on 3/19/2025, Respondent was to advise the Court and
DHS as to exactly what application(s) for relief Respondent was pursuing. It was confirmed on
5/27/2025 that Respondent's sole application is 42B.
4) Respondent's counsel confirmed Respondent's QRs are her three USC children: Christopher
age 16; George age 13 and Justin age 10.
5) The Court noted that the 1040 tax documents submitted by Respondent do not list each child
for each year.
6) The Court noted that there is no medical report detailing any of the QRs' diagnosis, course of
treatment or prognosis.
7) Respondent must submit specific evidence pursuant to Matter of J-J-G-, 27 I&N Dec. 808
(BIA 2020).
8) Experts may testify telephonically. Expert reports have less weight if the expert cannot be
cross examined.
9) Respondent confirmed that her best language is Spanish.

FOIA - ICE un mes
Corte Criminal - Aeropuerto donde lo detuvieron que fue en miami. 
Corte de immigration - miami
Esposa es ciudadana?

Client ask us the likelyyhood of detention. According to Client, he was found in 2006 with a fake work permit in an airport in Miami. He was detainied. He said that he was before a judge. We do not not if this judge was a IJ or a criminal court. This judge offered him a bond, he rejected and say he wanted to leave the country. He left, then he came back to the US and asked for asylum. He has a 130 pending. No legal entry due to his second entry. so he has to do consular processing. 

Due to the lack of complete information we cannot assert the likelihood of detention. It appears that the detention for the fake work permit was more than 10 years ago, and also that deprotation was 10 years ago. However, as a second rentry, this may appear to the official as innaceptable and want to jailed him. Despite any legal authoroty. So, it is more probable than not that he can be detained, we will need to check more info to give proper advisal.

Isued on 03/27/2026. The respondent shall file written pleadings within 30 days of the date of this order, unless
pleadings have already been entered or a deadline is otherwise specified by the immigration
judge. The entry of pleadings is a mandatory duty of the respondent and their counsel pursuant
to 8 C.F.R. § 1240.10(c).
2. Pleadings shall address each item set forth in Chapter 4.15(j) and Appendix K of the
Immigration Court Practice Manual (ICPM), including the estimated number of hours required
for the individual hearing and an interpreter request that clearly states the respondent’s preferred
language (and if the respondent’s preferred language is one with multiple variants, which variant
the respondent speaks).

21823.storyOfPersecution:
1992
│
├── June 15 → Born in the Dominican Republic
│
2019
│
├── Meets Jose Reyes → Relationship begins
│
2020 (Pandemic)
│
├── First verbal abuse → yelling, insults
│
Late 2021
│
├── Physical abuse begins
│
2021 – 2022
│
├── Ongoing physical violence (photos document abuse)
│
May 19, 2022
│
├── Sister witnesses abuse → Respondent slapped multiple times
│
August 11, 2022
│
├── Last encounter → Jose Reyes breaks into Respondent’s home
│
Sept – Oct 2022
│
├── Reports to police → No effective protection
│
October 12, 2022
│
├── Leaves the Dominican Republic. (BE CAREFULL WITH THE SIS/BRO AFFIDAVIT)
│
November 11, 2022
│
├── Enters the United States
│
March 7, 2024
│
└── Birth of daughter → U.S. citizen

caseNotesList.text: E-33 with 6301 Bay Park C1
Master Hearing
Client lives with his biological parents. He is no attending school, he is working as a electricist, in NJ, Carlos Vivar
21 year old
📎 Scheduling Order attached: memo_sanchez_legal_20260319.pdf (deadline: Mar 27, 2026) — Send oppossition brief and its evidence.

RELEVANT FACTS. A.Client Was Not Informed of the Deadline. Client, Mr. Luis Sanchez (Jr.), states that prior counsel of record -- Pilla & Gellman, P.C. / John Gregory Parilla (and/or Carolina Fernandez) -- never informed him of the February 17, 2026 call-up deadline. Client was therefore unaware that a corrected I-589 was due. B.Client Was Defrauded Prior to the Deadline. Before the February 17, 2026, deadline, client's father, Luis Sanchez, Sr., paid individuals who falsely held themselves out as attorneys. These individuals committed fraud against the client. Among other things, they requested that client provide fingerprints digitally. During a video call, a man wearing a uniform and an ICE badge instructed Luis Sanchez, Jr. to show his hands to the camera for digital fingerprinting. Client recognized this as fraudulent at that point. Client states he has video recordings of this call and emails documenting communications with these individuals. C.Romero Law Firm Entered the Record After the Deadline. The firm's E-28 was entered on February 19, 2026 -- two days after the deadline. The retainer was signed March 11, 2026. The firm needs additional time to review and verify all information before certifying the accuracy of the corrected I-589. III.STRATEGY -- OPPOSITION TO DHS MOTION TO PRETERMIT 1.Prior counsel did not notify client of the deadline. The February 17, 2026 call-up deadline was set by court order on September 23, 2025. Prior counsel of record (Pilla & Gellman / Parilla, and/or Carolina Fernandez) did not communicate this deadline to the client. Client's failure to comply was the result of counsel's failure to notify, not willful noncompliance. A sworn declaration from client will be submitted.2.Client actively sought counsel before the deadline but was defrauded. Prior to February 17, 2026, client's family paid individuals who fraudulently posed as attorneys. This conduct prevented client from obtaining legitimate representation within the deadline. Client has emails and video evidence to support this.3.Romero Law Firm entered the record two days after the deadline and requires additional time. Given the above circumstances, and the firm's need to verify all information in the corrected I-589, an equitable extension is warranted.4.DHS stated that the deadline was 02/19/2026. This is wrong, the 30 days deadline was on 02/17/2026. IV.PENDING TASKS A.Luis Sanchez, Jr. (Client) •Gather all emails with the fraudulent individuals and provide to Carlota. •Preserve all video recordings of the video call with the individual posing as ICE. •Obtain proof of payment made by Luis Sanchez, Sr. to the fraudulent individuals. •Prepare a chronology: exact dates of all contacts with prior counsel and with thefraudulent individuals. •Provide sworn declaration: (1) prior counsel did not inform him of the February 17, 2026deadline; and (2) full account of the fraud. Reference: Pilla & Gellman, P.C. / JohnGregory Parilla (and/or Carolina Fernandez). B. Carlota • Draft sworn declaration for client's review and signature. • Organize all evidentiary materials from client. C. Wilmer Gonzalez • Confirm Retainer date. • Draft written opposition to DHS motion to pretermit. V. SCHEDULING ORDER. [Scheduling order due by Friday 03/27/2026. Answer motion to pretermit]

Client sent screenshots of the conversations with an ICE agent and an attorney. El número 714 419  1100 es el supuesto agente de migración el me mandó un aviso que entre a la sala el zoom para las huellas dactilares . Virtuales . Y el otro número sería según el supuesto abogado

aster Calendar Hearing.  IJ,  Carrie J, found the I-589 "not adjudicable" due to unanswered questions.  IJ set a new Master Calendar Hearing for March 19, 2026, at 8:30 a.m., and issued a scheduling order requiring any and all relief to be submitted 30 days prior to the hearing.  Call-up deadline: February 17, 2026.  Counsel of record at the time: Pilla & Gellman, P.C. / John Gregory Parilla (Carolina Fernandez appeared at the Hearing. Not sure if she is part of the prior firm. She said on the record that she needed to send a motion to substitute counsel).
30-day call-up deadline.  No corrected I-589 was submitted.
Retainer agreement signed between client and the Romero Law Firm.
Romero Law Firm filed E-28 for Anibal Romero, two days after the deadline.

Master Calendar Hearing.  WG appeared and filed E-28 and corrected I-589 the same day.  IJ granted the motion to substitute counsel, leaving Anibal as primary counsel on the record.  DHS made an oral motion to pretermit the I-589 for noncompliance with the February 17, 2026, deadline.  IJ granted counsel 10 days to respond to the oral motion. [Scheduling order due by Friday 03/27/2026. Answer motion to pretermit]

📎 Scheduling Order attached: scheduling order due by may 14.pdf (deadline: May 15, 2026) — SO issued on 03/16/2026. Within 60 days of the date of this order, the respondent shall file any and all applications for
relief that have not yet been filed. a. Prima facie evidence of eligibility for all applications filed. b. Estimated number of hours required for the individual hearing. c. Requested format of respondent’s and counsel’s attendance (in-person or internet-based). d. Interpreter request that clearly states the respondent’s preferred language, and, if the respondent’s preferred language is one with multiple variants, which variant the respondent speaks.
e. A statement of whether the respondent intends to seek voluntary departure, either as the sole form of relief or in the alternative.

judges.motion: Wants for 360 approved: motion to dismiss, approved 360 + FBI fingerprints.
judges.master: Attends pro bono lawyers first. Put last 3 digits of number in the chat. Internet-based preferred in juvenile cases without a motion, but not in adult cases.
judges.individual: Does not require presence if child is under 14 or would miss a school day.
judges.name: Carrie Johnson-Papillo
Habib Fayaz
judges.master: 290 Broadway accepts Webex appearance. If there is an order to appear in person, ask for a Webex motion — he will approve it. State that you have stable connection and that Respondent will appear with Counsel.

judges.name: Kyle Dandelet
judges.notes: Was interested in the idea of the subpoena.

judges.name: Jem C. Sponzo
judges.motion: Opposes motion to terminate. Grants motion to dismiss based on 360.

judges.notes: Willing to admin closure if you can show prima facie eligibility for waiver.

Vivienne E. Gordon-Uruakpa

judges.name: ShaSha Xu
DIANA LOZADA RUIZ
judges.notes:
-Be warned, she is extremely difficult			
-Need to make very clear to the clients that they should try to get 6-4 Waivers of Service/Consent to Guardianship otherwise your case will drag on for a very long time			
-This judge is extremely particular and will not grant any motions to waive service if they are not satisfactory to her			
-Need to either get a 6-4 signed or personally serve the parent/parents because she will pick apart your motion to waive service if it is not "sufficiently detailed" to her liking

1) When did the child move to NY?
2) Where did the child enter the US?
3) How long has the child lived in NY?
4) Has the child been living continuously in NY?
5) Who are the adults who live in the house?
6) Do all parties consent to the case being heard by a referee? (Everyone said yes and the Referee had client and I sign a notice of stipulation to hear and determine)
